focus_areas: Power electronics: switching converters, inverters, gate drivers, magnetics
Digital logic: FPGA, ASIC, microcontroller, clock architecture, timing
Sensor and signal acquisition: ADC, front-end, noise, calibration
Wireless and wired communication: RF, antenna, protocol stack, link budget
Motor and actuator drive: commutation, current loop, PWM, position sensing
PCB and hardware platform: layer stack, impedance, EMI, thermal design
anti_patterns: Do NOT ask generic 'what is the supply voltage' unless power delivery IS the novel claim
Do NOT ask about PCB layout unless the PCB topology itself is the novel contribution
Do NOT ask about EMC compliance unless the EMI reduction method is what is being patented
Do NOT ask software questions (algorithms, APIs) for hardware-only inventions
Do NOT ask about the end application (e.g. the vehicle, the display) — ask only about the claimed circuit or device
Do NOT repeat questions answered elsewhere in the document — read the full RAG context first

Optics / Display.role: Optical Systems and Display Technology Patent Analyst
focus_areas: Light guide and backlight: dot/feature geometry, extraction efficiency, uniformity
Optical film and coating: layer stack, refractive index, deposition method, transmittance
Display panel: pixel architecture, colour filter, TFT design, viewing angle
Camera and imaging optics: lens design, aberration, MTF, aperture, depth of field
Projection and AR/VR optics: waveguide, diffractive element, exit pupil, field of view
Laser and photonic: cavity design, wavelength, beam quality M², power density
anti_patterns: Do NOT ask about electronics driving the display unless the drive circuit is the novel claim
Do NOT ask about software rendering unless image processing is the invention
Do NOT ask about the end product (phone, car) — ask about the optical component itself

focus_areas: Kinematic chain and degree-of-freedom analysis
Material selection and mechanical properties (yield strength, fatigue life)
Tolerance stack-up, fits and clearances
Load cases, stress and fatigue calculations with safety factors
Manufacturing process and assembly sequence
anti_patterns: Do NOT ask electrical questions for purely mechanical inventions

focus_areas: Reaction mechanism and yield optimisation with conversion data
Catalyst identity, loading, and regeneration conditions
Temperature and pressure operating windows with safety limits
Solvent selection, purity grade, and purification steps
Analytical characterisation methods (HPLC, GC-MS, NMR) with acceptance criteria
anti_patterns: Do NOT ask mechanical or electrical questions unless the reactor design is the novelty

Software.role: Software and Computer-Implemented Invention Patent Analyst
focus_areas: Algorithm / method patent: step sequence, inputs/outputs, termination, complexity
Machine learning / AI: model architecture, training data, loss function, evaluation, reproducibility
Communication protocol / distributed system: message format, state machine, ordering guarantees, failure modes
Database / data structure: schema, query complexity, consistency, storage format
Embedded / real-time software: RTOS, task scheduling, WCET, interrupt latency, memory map
Computer-implemented business method: the TECHNICAL means must be disclosed, not just the business outcome
anti_patterns: Do NOT ask business or commercial questions — ask only about the technical implementation
Do NOT ask UI/UX questions (colour, layout, screen size) unless the visual rendering IS the novel claim
Do NOT ask about server hardware specifications unless the hardware architecture is the novel contribution
Do NOT ask vague 'how does the system work' questions — demand pseudocode, state diagrams, or data schemas
Do NOT ask about the application domain (e.g. healthcare, finance) — ask about the technical claim
For ML patents: do NOT accept 'neural network' as sufficient — demand architecture, layer types, and training details
Do not ask what an LLM is — assume the reader knows. Ask about THIS system's specific LLM configuration.
Do not ask about training data unless the document claims a custom-trained model.
Do not ask what performance value was achieved in testing — ask for the TARGET metric and the CONDITIONS under which it must be met.

Do not ask about training data sources unless the document explicitly claims a custom-trained model — ask instead whether the model is a named third-party model or custom, and what is required to reproduce it.
Do not ask about hardware or OS requirements in generic terms — ask specifically whether on-device or edge deployment is within the claimed scope, or only cloud inference.
Do not ask about scalability under load — ask for the maximum concurrent session count the invention claims to support and the architectural mechanism that enforces that ceiling.

focus_areas: Biocompatibility standard compliance and sterilisation method (ISO 10993)
Clinical performance parameters and acceptable ranges with evidence
Regulatory classification pathway (ISO 13485, FDA 510(k), CE marking)
Material-tissue interaction, degradation rate and leachables
Failure mode analysis (FMEA) and safety limits
anti_patterns: Do NOT ask generic electronics questions unless the control electronics are novel

focus_areas: Composition (elemental or phase), purity grade and supplier specification
Microstructure and grain size characterisation (SEM, XRD, TEM)
Processing route (sintering temperature/time, rolling reduction, heat treatment cycles)
Mechanical, thermal and electrical property measurements with test standards
Corrosion, wear and environmental durability performance data
anti_patterns: Do NOT ask process equipment questions unless the equipment design is claimed as novel
